name: Functional Requirements Document

metadata.description: Defines detailed system behaviors, workflows, and functional interactions required to satisfy product features defined in the PRD.

sections.title: Functional Overview
sections.purpose: Provide a summary of the functional capabilities required to implement the product features.

sections.title: System Actors
sections.purpose: Identify the users, systems, and roles interacting with the system.

sections.title: Use Cases
sections.purpose: Describe user interactions with the system to achieve specific goals.

sections.title: User Workflows
sections.purpose: Define step-by-step flows describing how users interact with the system.

sections.title: Functional Requirements
sections.purpose: Provide a detailed list of system capabilities required to support product features.

sections.title: Input and Output Definitions
sections.purpose: Describe the expected data inputs and outputs for system interactions.

sections.title: Validation Rules
sections.purpose: Define business and system validation rules applied to inputs and workflows.

sections.title: State Transitions
sections.purpose: Describe lifecycle states and transitions of entities within the system.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.purpose: Define expected system behavior when failures or invalid operations occur.

sections.title: Audit and Logging Requirements
sections.purpose: Specify functional logging and auditing requirements where applicable.

sections.title: Functional Dependencies
sections.purpose: Identify dependencies on external systems, workflows, or operational processes.

sections.title: Approval and Sign Off
sections.purpose: Capture approval of the functional requirements.

validationRules: Each functional requirement must map to at least one PRD feature.
User workflows must correspond to defined use cases.
Validation rules must apply to at least one input or workflow.
State transitions must be defined for major entities.

promptHints.systemIntent: Generate a detailed functional requirements document describing system behaviors and workflows required to implement the product features.
rules: Do not introduce new product features not defined in the PRD.
Focus on system behavior rather than implementation details.
Clearly describe workflows and state transitions.
Ensure traceability between PRD features and functional requirements.
